title: Support `config`
body:
Hello!

This is not a problem with Verilator per se. But I'm trying to work around a fairly common use-case supported by other tools.  I'm running into many different problems. I'm hoping someone can point me to the right way to do this and then I can debug offline. 

- I have a testbench written in SystemVerilog with several submodules
- I have a dut also written in SystemVerilog with several submodules
- some of these submodules are shared between them. 
- however, the submodules may be versioned differently between the dut and testbench; different `defines, applied patches, etc.

With other simulators, I can specify which library to pick from using config.v files such as:

        config config_vlog;
            design testbench;
            instance testbench.dut liblist vloglib;
        endconfig


        config config_syn;
            design testbench;
            instance testbench.dut liblist pdklib synlib;
            // Use version with nonsynth delay
            cell bsg_rp_clk_gen_osc_v3_row use pdklib.bsg_rp_clk_gen_osc_v3_row;
        endconfig
 

I understand that Verilator does not / will not support config.v files:
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/master/docs/internals.rst#never-features

> IEEE 1800-2023 33 Config
> Little industry use.

I don't need super fine-grained support, so my workaround here is to try to do multiple verilations and then glue them together. For example's sake:


        [testbench.sv]
        /---------------------------------------------------\
        | testbench                                         |
        |   [driver.sv]                 [dut.sv]            |
        |      /---------------\          /---------------\ |
        |      | driver        |          | dut           | |
        |      |   [sub_v1.sv] |          |  [sub_v2.sv]  | |
        |      |     /-------\ |  <---->  |     /-------\ | |
        |      |     |  sub  | |          |     |  sub  | | |
        |      |     \-------/ |          |     \-------/ | |
        |      \---------------/          \---------------/ |
        \---------------------------------------------------/


I believe this could be supported by --lib-create / hierarchical Verilation. However, I would prefer to avoid this as based on (https://veripool.org/guide/latest/exe_verilator.html)
1) --timing is not supported
2) Signals in the block cannot be traced

I would like to do something like (pseudo-commands)

        verilate dut.sv sub_v2.sv -M obj_dir-dut
        verilate driver.sv sub_v1.sv -M obj_dir-driver
        verilate testbench.sv obj_dir-dut/*.a obj_dir-driver/*.a

What I've tried:
- this approach
  - can't get the linking to work properly during verilation, compilation errors
- verilating dut first, then verilating testbench.sv + driver.sv
  - links sub_v1 instead
- blackboxing sub_v2 and then separately verilating testbench.sv
  - could not get verilation to work for this either
- using --pipe-filter to prefix all modules in DUT
  - Mostly works but is not ideal for readability and debug. If all else fails, I'll probably pursue this more

So main questions:
- Has anyone solved this in a different way? A Verilator-specific compilation flow is completely fine but I would prefer to avoid any SV/C++ modifications incompatible with commercial tools.
- Is there anything missing in Verilator that fundamentally makes some version of libraries hard? I'm willing to work on a small port of this as it will be a fairly substantial remaining gap after other, harder, GLS support (UDP, SDF annotation) is merged.
- Is there a smaller subset of Verilator support (like individual module->file mapping) that would make this easier to implement?
- Any chance to reconsider basic implementation of IEEE 1800-2023.33 (a man can dream ;))

P.S. Is there a better place  (slack/element etc) to post long-winded questions? The Verilator "forum" in the README.md linked me here

Thank you!
